payload.reason: converged after 2 independent adversarial review rounds (both ran code; verified clean on real repo)

checklist.note: All of design §5 implemented: pure engine doc_refs.py (scope loader, backtick extraction, code-shape heuristic, source-id collection, scan_doc_refs), `doc refs` graded exits (warn exit 0 / --gate exit 2), 3 teeth (review approve block, done warn, CI doc-check.yml), and C-layer doc-impact checklist item. Implementer made a correctness improvement beyond plan Task 4: source-id set excludes src_exclude + doc_include (all .md), so top-level docs cannot self-resolve their own symbols — plan's literal Task 4 code would have had this bug.

7 non-test source/CI/doc files changed, all within the declared File-Structure scope; no stray edits. Two declared-but-unmodified files: AGENTS.md (correctly unaffected — it is a hand-curated narrative template, sync --check + sync --agents-md both clean, no diff) and private/OPEN-ITEMS.md (Task 11 deferred-work registration not done — minor, doc-only, non-blocking).

Reuses anchor_scanner._list_files/_matches_any/_excluded (git-aware walk cannot drift) and source_scope.load_source_scope; clear docstrings, frozen dataclass, deterministic sort, fail-open YAML loader matching house pattern. ruff + mypy clean, 1484 tests pass. Minor: done.py uses a lazy import of scan_doc_refs while review.py imports at top (harmless inconsistency).

Binary/unreadable files skipped (UnicodeDecodeError/PermissionError/OSError caught) in both collectors; corrupt/missing doc-paths.yaml falls back to defaults; docs/plans/** + examples/** excluded by default (verified examples/ exists in-repo); top-level *.md caught via explicit *.md glob (fnmatch **/*.md miss handled); output deterministic across runs; done warn wrapped in bare-except so it can never break done. Heuristic robust on adversarial set (camelCase aB/Db accepted, all-caps/short-lowercase/dotted/path/flag rejected); lone backtick `_` reads as symbol-shape but is a harmless non-risk since it always resolves.

This change's own doc surface is consistent: docs/cli-reference.md regenerated with the new `doc refs` section (doc check clean exit 0); AGENTS.md correctly unaffected (sync --check exit 0). The gate dogfoods itself — `doc refs --gate` on this repo exits 0, so the new CI step will not block its own PR. No stale doc claim introduced by the change.
